question: The sudden ______ of the local ecosystem can be attributed to the introduction of invasive species and rapid urban development.

stagnant

hazardously

alleviate

indifferent

because

since
moreover

question: The CEO ______ to resign after it was revealed that he had been involved in the embezzlement scandal.

question: Environmentalists are concerned ______ the impact of plastic pollution ______ marine life in the Pacific Ocean.

about / on
for / in

nor
and

so

submitted
submit
will submit
has submitted

on account of

Unlike

question: Although the team had spent months preparing for the expedition, ______.

the harsh weather conditions forced them to turn back

they have decided to publish their findings
it was a result of meticulous planning

question: ______ that many people are unaware of the impact their digital footprint has on their privacy.
choices: It is generally accepted
The fact
In spite of the news
Because it is reported
Unless the government acts
answer: It is generally accepted

question: Economic growth is often slower in developing nations ______.

because of a lack of infrastructure and investment
so that they can reach global markets

it was discovered in the late 20th century
nations must reduce their carbon emissions immediately
which is a vital part of the atmosphere

farmers have produced record yields

question: No sooner had the new law been passed ______.

than many companies began to change their policies
that the economy showed signs of recovery

answer: than many companies began to change their policies

question: Children who are exposed to multiple languages at a young age ______.

they can communicate in many ways

because their brains are still developing

answer: will likely find it easier to learn other languages later

question: The experimental drug showed promising results in the lab, ______.

but its long-term side effects are still unknown

answer: but its long-term side effects are still unknown

question: Just as the Industrial Revolution transformed the 19th century, ______.

steam engines were once the primary source of power
factories were built in many urban centers
so was the invention of the printing press
it had a profound impact on social structures
answer: the digital revolution is reshaping the 21st century

question: Despite the high costs associated with space exploration, ______.

it is a waste of resources for many countries
because it requires highly specialized equipment
the government has decided to cut the NASA budget
nations are reluctant to collaborate
answer: many argue that the benefits to technology outweigh the expenses

question: The decision to raise interest rates was taken to combat the rising inflation which has been affecting the country for months.
choices: Faiz oranlarını artırma kararı, aylardır ülkeyi etkileyen yükselen enflasyonla mücadele etmek için alındı.
Aylardır ülkeyi etkileyen enflasyon, faiz oranlarını artırma kararı alınmasına sebep oldu.
Enflasyonla mücadele etmek için hükümet aylardır faiz oranlarını artırmayı planlıyordu.
Ülkeyi aylardır etkileyen enflasyona karşı faiz oranlarının artırılması gerektiğine karar verildi.
Yükselen enflasyonla başa çıkmak için faiz oranlarının artırılması kararı geçen ay alındı.
answer: Faiz oranlarını artırma kararı, aylardır ülkeyi etkileyen yükselen enflasyonla mücadele etmek için alındı.

question: The museum, which houses thousands of artifacts from the Ottoman era, attracts millions of tourists every year.
choices: Her yıl milyonlarca turist çeken müzede Osmanlı döneminden binlerce eser bulunmaktadır.
Osmanlı dönemine ait binlerce esere ev sahipliği yapan müze, her yıl milyonlarca turist çekmektedir.
Müze, her yıl milyonlarca turistin ziyaret ettiği binlerce Osmanlı eserine sahiptir.
Milyonlarca turistin ilgisini çeken müzede binlerce yıllık Osmanlı eserleri sergilenmektedir.
Binlerce Osmanlı eserinin bulunduğu müze, her yıl turistlerin uğrak noktası olmaktadır.
answer: Osmanlı dönemine ait binlerce esere ev sahipliği yapan müze, her yıl milyonlarca turist çekmektedir.

question: Unless we find alternative energy sources soon, the current rate of consumption will lead to an energy crisis.
choices: Alternatif enerji kaynakları bulmadıkça, mevcut tüketim oranı bir enerji krizine yol açacaktır.
Eğer alternatif enerji kaynakları bulunmazsa, enerji krizi kaçınılmaz hale gelecektir.
Mevcut tüketim hızı sürdükçe yeni enerji kaynaklarına ihtiyaç duyulacaktır.
Enerji krizini önlemek için alternatif kaynakların en kısa sürede bulunması gerekir.
Alternatif enerji kaynakları bulunduğu takdirde mevcut tüketim oranı kriz yaratmayacaktır.
answer: Alternatif enerji kaynakları bulmadıkça, mevcut tüketim oranı bir enerji krizine yol açacaktır.

question: Son yıllarda yapılan araştırmalar, düzenli egzersizin sadece fiziksel sağlığı değil, aynı zamanda zihinsel sağlığı da iyileştirdiğini kanıtlamıştır.
choices: Recent studies have proved that regular exercise improves not only physical health but also mental health.
In recent years, it has been proved that regular exercise is beneficial for physical and mental health.
Studies conducted recently show that regular exercise improves mental health as much as physical health.
The link between physical health and mental health has been proved by recent exercise studies.
Regular exercise has been found to be the best way to improve mental and physical health in recent years.
answer: Recent studies have proved that regular exercise improves not only physical health but also mental health.

question: Yapay zeka teknolojisindeki hızlı gelişmeler, birçok iş kolunun gelecekte tamamen değişmesine neden olabilir.
choices: Rapid developments in artificial intelligence technology could cause many industries to change completely in the future.
The future of many jobs will depend on the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence technology.
Because of AI technology, many business sectors are expected to change in the near future.
Artificial intelligence is developing so fast that it might replace many jobs in the future.
Many industries might be forced to change due to the evolution of artificial intelligence technology.
answer: Rapid developments in artificial intelligence technology could cause many industries to change completely in the future.

question: Birleşmiş Milletler, dünya genelindeki yoksulluğu azaltmak amacıyla yeni bir sürdürülebilir kalkınma planı hazırladı.
choices: The United Nations has prepared a new sustainable development plan with the aim of reducing poverty worldwide.
A new plan for sustainable development has been prepared by the United Nations to end global poverty.
To reduce poverty across the world, the United Nations is planning a new sustainable development program.
The United Nations aims to reduce world poverty through its recently prepared development plan.
Sustainable development is the goal of the new plan prepared by the United Nations for the poor.
answer: The United Nations has prepared a new sustainable development plan with the aim of reducing poverty worldwide.

question: Passage 1: The Anthropocene epoch is a proposed geological period dating from the commencement of significant human impact on Earth's geology and ecosystems. While the term was coined in the 1960s, it gained widespread prominence in 2000 through the work of Paul Crutzen. The primary argument for this new designation is that human activities, particularly since the Industrial Revolution, have altered the carbon cycle, nitrogen cycle, and global climate to such an extent that they leave a permanent mark in the lithosphere. However, critics argue that the term is more political than scientific, suggesting that the Holocene—the current epoch—already encompasses human civilization. According to the passage, the primary reason for proposing the Anthropocene is...
choices: The exhaustion of natural resources during the Holocene
The significant and permanent impact of human activities on Earth's systems
The discovery of new geological layers in the 1960s
The political need to regulate industrial emissions
A desire to redefine the Industrial Revolution's timeline
answer: The significant and permanent impact of human activities on Earth's systems

question: Passage 1: [Text as above] It can be inferred from the passage that Paul Crutzen...
choices: Was the first scientist to observe the nitrogen cycle
Discovered the lithosphere in the late 20th century
Played a crucial role in popularizing the concept of the Anthropocene
Opposes the idea that human activity affects the global climate
Argues that the Holocene ended before the Industrial Revolution
answer: Played a crucial role in popularizing the concept of the Anthropocene

question: Passage 1: [Text as above] The word 'prominence' in the text is closest in meaning to...
choices: Complexity
Notoriety
Resistance
Fame
Uncertainty
answer: Fame

question: Passage 1: [Text as above] What is one of the arguments used by critics of the Anthropocene designation?
choices: Human activities have no impact on the lithosphere
The Holocene already covers the period of human civilization
Geological epochs cannot be named after human activities
The carbon cycle has not changed significantly since 2000
Paul Crutzen's work lacks scientific data
answer: The Holocene already covers the period of human civilization

question: Passage 2: Behavioral economics challenges the traditional economic assumption that humans are rational actors who always seek to maximize their utility. Instead, it incorporates insights from psychology to explain why people often make systematic errors in judgment. Concepts such as loss aversion—the idea that the pain of losing is twice as powerful as the joy of gaining—and the endowment effect demonstrate that our decisions are frequently driven by cognitive biases rather than logic. By understanding these irrationalities, policymakers can design 'nudges' that steer people toward better choices without restricting their freedom. The passage implies that traditional economics...
choices: Is completely invalid in modern society
Relies on the assumption of human rationality
Focuses primarily on the psychology of loss
Encourages people to make systematic errors
Has always used 'nudges' to influence behavior
answer: Relies on the assumption of human rationality

question: Passage 2: [Text as above] According to the passage, 'loss aversion' refers to...
choices: The desire to avoid all economic risks
The tendency to value gains more than losses
The psychological phenomenon where losses felt more intensely than equivalent gains
A systematic error where people refuse to spend money
The strategy of maximizing utility through logical choices
answer: The psychological phenomenon where losses felt more intensely than equivalent gains

question: Passage 2: [Text as above] What is the purpose of 'nudges' mentioned in the text?
choices: To force individuals to obey government regulations
To correct the endowment effect in the stock market
To guide people toward beneficial decisions while maintaining choice
To eliminate the need for psychology in economic theory
To maximize the pain of losing for consumers
answer: To guide people toward beneficial decisions while maintaining choice

question: Passage 2: [Text as above] Which of the following would be an example of a systematic error in judgment as described in the passage?
choices: Calculating the exact interest rate on a loan
Buying a product only because you already feel a sense of ownership over it
Choosing the cheapest option available in a store
Diversifying a portfolio to minimize risk
Selling an asset when its value reaches a peak
answer: Buying a product only because you already feel a sense of ownership over it

question: Passage 3: The Library of Alexandria was one of the largest and most significant libraries of the ancient world. Dedicated to the Muses, it functioned as a major center for scholarship from its construction in the 3rd century BC until the Roman conquest of Egypt. The library's goal was to collect all the world's knowledge, and at its height, it may have held hundreds of thousands of papyrus scrolls. However, its destruction is a subject of historical debate, with theories blaming Julius Caesar, the Caliph Omar, or internal decay and budget cuts. Regardless of the cause, its loss represents a profound gap in our understanding of ancient history. The main focus of the passage is...
choices: The specific number of scrolls lost in Alexandria
The architectural design of ancient libraries
The historical importance and mysterious disappearance of the Library of Alexandria
The life of Julius Caesar in Egypt
The translation of papyrus scrolls into Latin
answer: The historical importance and mysterious disappearance of the Library of Alexandria

question: Passage 3: [Text as above] According to the passage, the library's ultimate objective was...
choices: To serve as a temple for Roman gods
To store only the records of the Egyptian pharaohs
To compile the entirety of the world's knowledge
To compete with the libraries of Rome
To protect papyrus from physical decay
answer: To compile the entirety of the world's knowledge

question: Passage 3: [Text as above] Why is the destruction of the library considered a 'profound gap'?
choices: Because we no longer know how to make papyrus
Because it resulted in the loss of irreplaceable historical records
Because it led to the fall of the Roman Empire
Because it was the only library in the ancient world
Because its location has never been found
answer: Because it resulted in the loss of irreplaceable historical records

question: Passage 3: [Text as above] Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a possible cause for the library's end?
choices: A fire caused by Julius Caesar
A lack of funding and internal decline
The invasion of a Caliph
An earthquake that submerged the city
The Roman conquest of Egypt
answer: An earthquake that submerged the city

question: Passage 4: Dark matter is a hypothetical form of matter that is thought to account for approximately 85% of the matter in the universe. Its presence is implied in a variety of astrophysical observations, including gravitational effects that cannot be explained by accepted theories of gravity unless more matter is present than can be seen. Because dark matter does not appear to interact with the electromagnetic field, it does not absorb, reflect, or emit light, making it extremely difficult to detect. Astronomers infer its existence from the motion of stars and galaxies. Scientists are currently using particle accelerators and deep underground detectors to identify the particles that constitute it. Based on the passage, dark matter is described as 'hypothetical' because...
choices: Most scientists do not believe it exists
It has been proven to be a mathematical error
It has not been directly observed or identified yet
It only exists in the proximity of black holes
It was only recently discovered by particle accelerators
answer: It has not been directly observed or identified yet

question: Passage 4: [Text as above] How do astronomers 'see' dark matter?
choices: By using powerful telescopes that detect infrared light
By observing how it reflects light from distant stars
Through its gravitational influence on visible celestial bodies
By measuring the electromagnetic waves it emits
By identifying it in the lithosphere of planets
answer: Through its gravitational influence on visible celestial bodies

question: Passage 4: [Text as above] What makes dark matter particularly elusive to detect?
choices: It is located outside the known universe
It does not interact with light or electromagnetism
It moves faster than the speed of light
It is only found in the deep underground
It has no mass and thus no gravity
answer: It does not interact with light or electromagnetism

question: Passage 4: [Text as above] It can be concluded from the passage that...
choices: The majority of matter in the universe is visible to the naked eye
Current theories of gravity are sufficient without dark matter
Scientific efforts are ongoing to find the physical components of dark matter
Dark matter will eventually replace all normal matter
Particle accelerators have already successfully captured dark matter
answer: Scientific efforts are ongoing to find the physical components of dark matter

question: Passage 5: The concept of 'biophilia' suggests that humans possess an innate tendency to seek connections with nature and other forms of life. Edward O. Wilson, who popularized the term, argued that this bond is a product of biological evolution. For most of human history, our ancestors lived in close contact with the natural world, and those who could read nature's cues were more likely to survive. Today, research in environmental psychology supports this, showing that access to green spaces can reduce stress and improve cognitive function. Despite urbanization, the psychological need for nature remains a fundamental part of the human condition. What is the central thesis of the biophilia hypothesis?
choices: Urbanization is the primary cause of human evolution
Humans have an inherent biological connection to the natural world
Nature is a dangerous force that humans must control
Cognitive function is independent of environmental factors
Only trained biologists can truly connect with nature
answer: Humans have an inherent biological connection to the natural world

question: Passage 5: [Text as above] According to E.O. Wilson, the biophilic bond developed because...
choices: It helped our ancestors survive by understanding their environment
Ancient religions mandated a love for animals
Modern cities are too stressful for the human brain
Plants produce chemicals that change human DNA
It was a requirement for the Industrial Revolution
answer: It helped our ancestors survive by understanding their environment

question: Passage 5: [Text as above] How does modern research support the biophilia hypothesis?
choices: By proving that humans no longer need nature
By showing the therapeutic benefits of green spaces on mental health
By developing new technologies to replace natural environments
By studying the evolution of urban architecture
By demonstrating that urbanization has eliminated the need for nature
answer: By showing the therapeutic benefits of green spaces on mental health

question: Passage 5: [Text as above] The word 'innate' in the first paragraph most nearly means...
choices: Acquired
Learned
Inborn
Temporary
Cultural
answer: Inborn

question:
Kevin: I’ve been reading about Universal Basic Income lately. It seems like a radical way to address poverty.
Sarah: It is, but many economists worry about the inflation it might trigger.
Kevin: __________________
Sarah: That’s a fair point, but finding a balance between support and economic stability is the real challenge.
choices: Inflation is inevitable regardless of what the government does.
But wouldn't the increase in consumer spending boost the economy in the long run?
I think we should just lower taxes instead.
I don't believe poverty can ever be truly eliminated.
Why are economists always so pessimistic about social welfare?
answer: But wouldn't the increase in consumer spending boost the economy in the long run?

question:
Professor: The deadline for the dissertation proposal is next Friday. No exceptions.
Student: __________________
Professor: I understand, but the schedule is tight because of the upcoming symposium. Try to focus on the core thesis for now.
choices: I have already finished my research, so I am ready.
Will we be graded on the symposium as well?
Is there any way I could get a short extension due to my illness?
Can I change my topic after the deadline?
I am planning to submit it by Wednesday.
answer: Is there any way I could get a short extension due to my illness?

question:
Journalist: Minister, how does the government plan to handle the rising energy costs this winter?
Minister: We are implementing a series of subsidies for low-income households.
Journalist: __________________
Minister: We are currently negotiating with international suppliers to diversify our energy sources.
choices: Will those subsidies be enough to cover the increase?
That sounds good, but what about the long-term energy security?
Energy costs are rising all over the world, aren't they?
Why didn't you invest in solar energy ten years ago?
When will the subsidies be distributed?
answer: That sounds good, but what about the long-term energy security?

question:
Client: I'm not sure if this software update is worth the cost.
Consultant: It includes advanced security protocols that protect against the latest cyber threats.
Client: __________________
Consultant: Precisely. A single data breach could cost you much more than the update fee.
choices: I think my current firewall is sufficient.
Is the interface easier to use than the old version?
So, you're saying it's more of a preventative investment?
How long will the installation process take?
Can I get a discount if I buy multiple licenses?
answer: So, you're saying it's more of a preventative investment?

question:
Amy: Did you see the latest findings on the Mars rover mission?
Mark: Yes, the evidence of ancient organic molecules is groundbreaking.
Amy: __________________
Mark: Exactly. It doesn't prove there was life, but it shows the ingredients were there.
choices: I wonder if we will ever send humans to Mars.
It brings us one step closer to understanding if Mars was once habitable.
The rover mission has been very expensive for taxpayers.
I think the moon mission was more significant.
Organic molecules are common in the universe, though.
answer: It brings us one step closer to understanding if Mars was once habitable.

question: Despite the fierce competition in the smartphone market, the company managed to increase its market share by focusing on niche consumer needs.
choices: The company failed to grow because the competition in the smartphone market was too intense.
By targeting specific customer requirements, the company expanded its share of the market even though competition was tough.
The company decided to ignore the competition and focus only on high-end luxury smartphones.
Market share increase was inevitable for the company due to its focus on niche products despite no competition.
The fierce competition in the market led the company to abandon its focus on niche consumers.
answer: By targeting specific customer requirements, the company expanded its share of the market even though competition was tough.

question: Hardly had the new law been enacted when the public began to voice their dissatisfaction through various protests.
choices: The public protested for a long time before the new law was finally passed.
As soon as the new law came into effect, people started expressing their unhappiness via protests.
The new law was enacted because the public had been protesting for months.
The government expected protests, so they delayed the enactment of the law.
Dissatisfaction with the law was only expressed after it had been in place for several years.
answer: As soon as the new law came into effect, people started expressing their unhappiness via protests.

question: It is not so much the lack of talent as the lack of persistence that prevents many artists from achieving success.
choices: Many artists fail to succeed because they are neither talented nor persistent.
Talent is far more important than persistence for an artist who wants to be successful.
Most artists fail because they don't have enough talent, regardless of how persistent they are.
The failure of many artists to succeed is due more to their lack of perseverance than a lack of ability.
If an artist is talented enough, they do not need to be persistent to achieve success.
answer: The failure of many artists to succeed is due more to their lack of perseverance than a lack of ability.

question: Were it not for the timely intervention of the rescue teams, the casualties from the flood would have been much higher.
choices: The rescue teams arrived too late to prevent a high number of casualties during the flood.
Even with the rescue teams' help, the number of casualties remained significantly high.
The flood caused many deaths because the rescue teams were unable to intervene.
If the rescue teams had not intervened when they did, more people would have died in the flood.
The rescue teams intervened only after the number of casualties had already peaked.
answer: If the rescue teams had not intervened when they did, more people would have died in the flood.

question: The concept of sustainable development has evolved significantly since the 1980s. Initially, it was seen primarily as an environmental issue, focusing on the conservation of natural resources. __________________. Today, it is understood as a multidimensional approach that balances economic growth, social equity, and environmental protection.
choices: However, the Industrial Revolution changed the way we perceive nature.
Furthermore, many countries still refuse to sign environmental treaties.
However, over time, the definition expanded to include social and economic dimensions.
This led to the immediate cessation of all deforestation worldwide.
Environmentalists argue that the economy should be the only priority.
answer: However, over time, the definition expanded to include social and economic dimensions.

question: In the realm of quantum mechanics, particles can exist in a state of superposition. This means that a particle can be in multiple states at the same time until it is measured. __________________. Once an observation is made, the wave function collapses, and the particle settles into a single, definite state.
choices: This phenomenon is the basis for traditional computing systems.
Measurement, therefore, plays a fundamental role in determining reality at the quantum level.
Albert Einstein was the first to successfully measure a particle in superposition.
Most particles are too large to be affected by these quantum laws.
The collapse of the wave function is a myth that has been debunked by modern physics.
answer: Measurement, therefore, plays a fundamental role in determining reality at the quantum level.

question: The rise of remote work has fundamentally altered the corporate landscape. Companies have realized that employees can be just as productive, if not more so, from their homes. __________________. Consequently, many firms are downsizing their physical office spaces to reduce overhead costs.
choices: Commuting to work is still considered the most efficient way to start the day.
This shift has led to a decrease in the demand for commercial real estate in city centers.
Employees often prefer to work in loud, collaborative environments.
Remote work is only possible for those in the manufacturing sector.
The internet has little to do with the success of remote working models.
answer: This shift has led to a decrease in the demand for commercial real estate in city centers.

question: Standardized testing has long been a staple of the educational system, intended to provide an objective measure of student achievement. __________________. Critics argue that these tests often measure a student's ability to memorize facts rather than their critical thinking or creativity.
choices: However, its efficacy and fairness have come under increasing scrutiny in recent years.
Therefore, all universities should rely solely on these test scores for admissions.
Students who score well on these tests are guaranteed to succeed in their careers.
The history of standardized testing dates back to ancient China.
